name.context: A small, sustainable house built in a rural area.

country.context: The UK is mentioned as the location for this study.

year_of_construction.context: This house was built in 2005 and is used as a case study.

design_strategies.name: Green wall system
design_strategies.anchor_text: Green walls are a common feature in sustainable buildings.

design_strategies.name: Rainwater harvesting systems
design_strategies.anchor_text: The building features a rainwater harvesting system to collect and store rainwater for non-potable uses.

design_strategies.name: Greywater treatment
design_strategies.anchor_text: The building has a greywater treatment system to reuse water for irrigation and flushing toilets.

design_strategies.name: Biophilic design
design_strategies.anchor_text: The building's design incorporates biophilic principles to promote occupant well-being and productivity.

design_strategies.name: Green façade
design_strategies.anchor_text: The building's exterior features a green façade to reduce urban heat island effects and improve air quality.

ecosystem_services.name: Primary production
ecosystem_services.anchor_text: forms the basis of the planet's food chain

ecosystem_services.name: Regulation of air quality
ecosystem_services.anchor_text: regulate the concentrations of gases in the Earth's atmosphere
